messages.content:
Сделай рерайт на английском "On Willem-Alexander and Maxima’s wedding day—February 2, 2002—the new Dutch princess wore a reworked version of a classic tiara from the family vaults. The Dutch Star Tiara combines the base of the Pearl Button Tiara with five ten-pointed diamond stars that belonged to Queen Emma of the Netherlands. Maxima wore this tiara often in the early years of her marriage, but it’s rarer to see her wear it now.
So far, only three members of the Dutch royal family have worn the star version of this tiara: Máxima, Princess Beatrix (who wears it above at a diplomatic reception in 2015), and Princess Margriet. It’s not a tiara that every princess could pull off — you need an element of pizzazz about you to make the stars work. But it’s practically perfect for Máxima, who absolutely oozes joie de vivre."

On their wedding day, February 2, 2002, the then-future Dutch princess, Maxima, sported an updated version of a traditional tiara from the royal collection. The Dutch Star Tiara, a fusion of the Pearl Button Tiara base with five ten-pointed diamond stars from Queen Emma of the Netherlands, adorned her head. In her early marriage years, Maxima frequently chose this tiara, though it has become less common for her to wear it nowadays.

Up until now, just three members of the Dutch royal family have been seen wearing this starry tiara variation: Máxima, Princess Beatrix (spotted in it at a diplomatic event in 2015), and Princess Margriet. This particular tiara isn't suited for just any princess; it demands a certain flair to truly shine. However, for Máxima, whose presence is bursting with a lively spirit, it seems to be an impeccable match.

сделай краткий пересказ "this Tiara can be worn with or without the pearls, the largest four of which  belonged to Princess Amalia of Orange née Soms-Braunfels, who was the Princess of Orange between 1625 and 1647, and the Regent between 1650 and 1672.
The Antique Pearl Tiara was commissioned by Queen Wilhelmina between 1900 and 1910, based on the design of a similar Tiara belonging to her grandmother, Queen Anna Pavlovna.
Princess Beatrix became the primary wearer of the Antique Pearl Tiara from the late 1950s, with it entering her usual rotation alongside the Württemberg Ornate Pearl Tiara and Queen Emma’s Diamond Tiara. 
Queen Beatrix continued to wear the Antique Pearl Taira regularly after her accession to the Throne in 1980.
Following her abdication, Princess Beatrix has continued to wear the Antique Pearl Tiara, with notable appearances during the Argentine State Visit to The Netherlands in 2017, the Cape Verde State Visit to The Netherlands in 2018, and the Indian State Visit to The Netherlands in 2022.
Queen Máxima has worn the Antique Pearl Tiara rather regularly over the past few years, for a Chinese State Banquet in 2014, at the Diplomatic Corps Gala Dinner at the Royal Palace of Amsterdam in 2019, during the Dutch State Visit to Austria in 2022, and most recently during the French State Visit to The Netherlands in 2023.
Maxima has also worn the base of the Antique Pearl Tiara without its pearl toppers—in fact, this smaller setting was the very first tiara she wore in public. She debuted the small diamond sparkler way back in August 2001, when she was still Maxima Zorreguieta, at the wedding of Crown Prince Haakon and Crown Princess Mette-Marit of Norway. The photo above was taken in March 2003, during a state visit from President Ciampi of Italy to the Netherlands.
"

The Antique Pearl Tiara, boasting pearls that originally belonged to Princess Amalia of Orange from the 17th century, was created for Queen Wilhelmina in the early 20th century, inspired by a design from Queen Anna Pavlovna. Princess Beatrix began frequently wearing this tiara in the late 1950s, incorporating it into her signature royal accessories. She continued its regular use throughout her reign as Queen and even after abdicating, showcasing it at various state visits up to 2022. Queen Máxima has also embraced the tiara, donning it at numerous official events in recent years, including state visits and gala dinners. Significantly, Máxima has been seen wearing the tiara's base without the pearls, starting from 2001 before she married into the Dutch royal family.

сделай рерайт, немного сократив текст "This simple diamond bandeau belongs to the Dutch royal family. When it came into the family, however, it wasn’t as a tiara, but a necklace. In 1879 the people of the Netherlands gave Princess Emma of Waldeck and Pyrmont a large diamond necklace and brooch for her wedding to King William III. Her daughter, Queen Wilhelmina, would be the one to take 34 of the largest diamonds from the necklace and have them set in a platinum frame. The tiara was first seen on then-Princess Juliana, Wilhelmina’s daughter, in 1937.
Since the bandeau’s creation it has been worn by Queens Wilhelmina, Juliana, Beatrix, and Maxima, as well as Princesses Margriet and Christina. The Dutch Diamond Bandeau is currently part of the Orange-Nassau Family Collection, insuring that it stays with the family. While this is probably one of the simplest tiara’s you’ll see in a royal collection, it sure packs a punch. The carat power in this bandeau rivals that of other larger, more complex tiaras. Personally, I love the way Maxima wears this tiara. Always highlighting and complementing her ensemble, perfectly coiffed hair, just so elegant. A beautiful tiara for a beautiful woman.
"

This elegant diamond bandeau is a cherished piece of the Dutch royal family's jewelry collection. Originally presented as a large diamond necklace and brooch to Princess Emma of Waldeck and Pyrmont in 1879 by the Dutch people during her marriage to King William III, it was Queen Wilhelmina who transformed it into a tiara. She repurposed 34 of the necklace's largest diamonds, setting them in a platinum bandeau first worn by her daughter, Princess Juliana, in 1937. Throughout the years, it has adorned the heads of Queens Wilhelmina, Juliana, Beatrix, and Maxima, as well as Princesses Margriet and Christina, ensuring its place in the Orange-Nassau Family Collection. Despite its simplicity, the Dutch Diamond Bandeau is a powerful piece of royal attire, boasting impressive carat weight. Queen Maxima, in particular, is known for wearing it with unparalleled elegance, perfectly complementing her outfits and sophisticated hairstyles. It stands as a testament to beauty and simplicity within the royal jewelry collection.

Her daughter, Queen Wilhelmina, repurposed 34 of the necklace’s largest diamonds, setting them in a platinum frame. The tiara was first seen on then-Princess Juliana, Wilhelmina’s daughter, in 1937.

 Throughout the years, it has adorned the heads of Queens Wilhelmina, Juliana, Beatrix, and Maxima, as well as Princesses Margriet and Christina. In 1879, the people of the Netherlands gifted Princess Emma of Waldeck and Pyrmont a large diamond necklace and brooch for her wedding to King William III. Her daughter, Queen Wilhelmina, repurposed 34 of the necklace’s largest diamonds, setting them in a platinum frame. The tiara was first seen on then-Princess Juliana, Wilhelmina’s daughter, in 1937.

do rewrite "the Dutch Diamond Festoon Tiara. A necklace of five diamond festoons, which are able to be removed from the rest of the necklace, placed on a frame, and worn as a tiara. Made by Vita. Part of a collection of diamond jewels presented to Queen Wilhelmina on her ninth birthday by her father, King Willem III, in 1889. 
The arrival of Queen Maxima breathed new life into the diamond jewel. She was the first member of the royal family to wear the necklace in public on a tiara frame. Since then, the tiara version of the jewel has also been worn by Princess Margriet.
Now part of the family's jewel foundation.
"

The Dutch Diamond Festoon Tiara is a versatile masterpiece, originally a necklace comprising five removable diamond festoons that can be affixed to a frame and transformed into a tiara. Created by Vita, this piece was part of a diamond jewelry collection gifted to Queen Wilhelmina by her father, King Willem III, for her ninth birthday in 1889.

Queen Maxima brought renewed attention to this exquisite piece, being the first royal to don the necklace as a tiara in a public setting. Following her lead, Princess Margriet has also worn the tiara rendition of this jewel. It is now a cherished asset within the royal family's jewel foundation.

do rewrite "The diamond hair ornament or hair comb is in a fringe-like design. The top row diamonds are detachable and other pendants or jewels can be attached.
The piece originally belonged to Queen Wilhelmina and was presented to the Queen by her husband, Prince Hendrik, in late 1910s, along with several other pieces of jewellery.
This piece hadn't been seen since 1920s and some assumed it was either sold, or broken apart to create new jewels.
And then the hairpiece graced Princess Maxima’s head in June of 2010, during the pre-wedding celebrations of Crown Princess Victoria and Prince Daniel. 
The next appearance was almost two years later, during state visit to Singapore on January 24, 2013; this time, Maxima wore it as a tiara.
"

This fringe-like diamond hair comb, capable of being customized with detachable top row diamonds to which other gems or pendants can be affixed, initially graced the collection of Queen Wilhelmina. It was a gift from her husband, Prince Hendrik, in the late 1910s among other jewelry items.

After disappearing from the public eye since the 1920s, there was speculation that it had either been sold or dismantled for new jewelry creations. However, the hair ornament made a notable reappearance on Princess Maxima during the pre-wedding festivities for Crown Princess Victoria and Prince Daniel in June 2010. It was seen again nearly two years later, this time reimagined as a tiara by Maxima, during a state visit to Singapore on January 24, 2013.

Сделай лаконичный рерайт, чтобы максимально понятно и правильно звучало для носителей языка "The Dutch Emerald Parure Tiara was created using family emeralds in 1899 and given to Queen Wilhelmina as a present from her mother, Queen Emma. Originally there were six emeralds on the tiara, however, two were removed and repurposed to make a set of earrings.
The popular tiara is versatile and can also be worn with the emeralds replaced by diamonds or pearls. Princess Annemarie wore the pearl setting for her 2010 wedding.
These days, Queen Maxima tends to wear the tiara most frequently in its emerald form.
Maxima has also worn the second pearl setting of the Emerald Parure Tiara. This version of the tiara features five pear-shaped pearls placed upright on the sparkler. 
"

The Dutch Emerald Parure Tiara, crafted with family emeralds in 1899, was a gift to Queen Wilhelmina from her mother, Queen Emma. Initially featuring six emeralds, two were later used to create earrings. This adaptable tiara can be worn with diamonds or pearls instead of emeralds. Princess Annemarie chose the pearl version for her 2010 wedding. Queen Maxima frequently sports the tiara in its emerald configuration but has also showcased a second pearl setting, distinguished by five pear-shaped pearls.

"The Mellerio Ruby Parure tiara is the second magnificent diamond and ruby tiara to belong to the Dutch royal family. The tiara was created by the jewellery brand Mellerio dits Meller for Queen Emma of the Netherlands in 1889.
It forms part of a larger ‘Ruby’ collection, all acquired by King Willem III for his wife at the same time. It has been worn by all four Queens since Queen Emma, and was chosen as the tiara for the first official portrait of Queen Máxima in 2013.
"

The Mellerio Ruby Parure Tiara, an exquisite piece featuring diamonds and rubies, is a notable ornament within the Dutch royal family's collection. Crafted by Mellerio dits Meller in 1889 for Queen Emma of the Netherlands, this tiara is a key element of a broader 'Ruby' collection purchased by King Willem III for his wife. Since Queen Emma, all subsequent queens have adorned this tiara. Most notably, it was selected for Queen Máxima's first official portrait in 2013.

"the Dutch Sapphire Parure Tiara,
this glittering Gothic-style headpiece is one of the largest in the Dutch royal collection. It features 655 diamonds and 33 sapphires, with the central sapphire weighing at roughly 44 carats.
Initially, it was set on a frame that allowed it to be broken into parts, including a haircomb and a brooch made out of the central sapphire element. However, in 1928, the Dutch court jeweler Van Kempen made alterations making it so that the piece was composed of one single golden frame, rather than small detachable ones. 
The tiara was made in 1881 as a gift for Queen Emma from her husband King Willem III.
In 2013, ahead of the inauguration of King Willem-Alexander, the base of the tiara was supplemented so that the piece now sits slightly higher on the wearer’s head. Queen Maxima wore the tiara for the first time in public at her husband’s inauguration, but on that occasion, she didn’t actually wear the complete piece. The tall central diamond plume of the tiara was removed for the event and replaced with a single round brilliant."

The Dutch Sapphire Parure Tiara stands out as a majestic Gothic-style piece within the Dutch royal jewels, adorned with 655 diamonds and 33 sapphires, including a central sapphire of approximately 44 carats. Originally constructed in 1881 as a gift from King Willem III to Queen Emma, the tiara initially featured a modular frame, allowing components to be reconfigured into items like a haircomb and a brooch. In 1928, alterations by Van Kempen transformed it into a singular golden frame for a cohesive structure.

Before King Willem-Alexander's 2013 inauguration, the tiara's base was modified to elevate its positioning on the head. Queen Maxima debuted it publicly at the ceremony, opting for a simplified version by replacing the prominent central diamond feature with a single round brilliant, rather than wearing the tiara in its full original splendor.

"Württemberg Ornate Pearl Tiara
This tiara was made in 1897 by Van Kempen & Begeer using pearls from the Orange-Nassau collection. It can be worn in four different settings; with two rows of pear shaped pearls, none of the pear shaped pearls, just the top row of pearls, or just the bottom row of pearls. Maxima has yet to wear the complete tiara.
"

The Württemberg Ornate Pearl Tiara, crafted in 1897 by Van Kempen & Begeer, incorporates pearls from the Orange-Nassau collection. This versatile piece offers four distinct configurations: featuring both rows of pear-shaped pearls, without any pear-shaped pearls, with just the top row, or solely the bottom row of pearls. As of now, Maxima has not been seen wearing the tiara in its full arrangement.

"the Stuart Tiara
Perhaps the grandest Dutch royal tiara of all is the Stuart Tiara.
Made in 1897, this spectacular headpiece is named for the 39-carat pear-cut Stuart diamond, which serves as its centrepiece. The historic diamond had been in the Dutch royal family since 1690.
The Stuart tiara was first worn by Queen Wilhelmina, who wore it at her investiture in 1898 and her wedding in 1901.
 Maxima initially wore a smaller version of the tiara in public, but in October 2018, during a state visit to the United Kingdom, she wore the full tiara for a state banquet at Buckingham Palace.
Thank you for watching this video. Share your impressions in the comments and support my channel by subscribing and liking. Thank you.
"

The Stuart Tiara stands as one of the most magnificent pieces within the Dutch royal tiara collection. Crafted in 1897, it is distinguished by the 39-carat pear-cut Stuart diamond at its heart, a gem that has been part of the Dutch royal heritage since 1690. Queen Wilhelmina was the first to don this exquisite tiara, wearing it for her investiture in 1898 and her wedding in 1901. While Maxima initially appeared in a scaled-down version of the tiara, she presented it in its full glory during a state visit to the United Kingdom in October 2018, wearing it to a state banquet at Buckingham Palace. Thank you for engaging with this video. Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments, and if you enjoyed the content, please support the channel by subscribing and liking.
